message:
security(interactive): treat empty/unrecognized scope input as cancel, not allow

`select_allowlist_scope` is the prompt that follows the
"command blocked, choose allow scope" denial UI. The denial header
states explicitly:

    [Enter] = Keep blocked / cancel

— which the user reads as "hitting Enter without typing anything is
the safe default; the command stays blocked." But the prompt's match
arms were:

    "" => Ok(AllowlistScope::Once),
    _  => Ok(AllowlistScope::Once),

Both empty input and any unrecognized input were silently coerced to
`AllowlistScope::Once` — a single-shot allow that bypasses the gate
and runs the dangerous command exactly once. For commands gated only
by `VerificationMethod::None` (the most common dcg case), this prompt
is the LAST line of defense before execution. Defaulting to allow on
Enter turned the documented "Enter = keep blocked" UX into a
hands-free allow.

The fix is to return `Err(InputError::Interrupted)` for both arms.
The caller (`run_interactive_prompt`) maps that error to
`InteractiveResult::Cancelled`, which the evaluator treats the same
as an explicit `n` denial: the command stays blocked and dcg exits
with the standard non-zero hook exit so the user's `Bash` invocation
fails.

Net effect: hitting Enter at the scope prompt now does what the
denial header says it does. Typing a bogus scope (typo, pasted junk,
keystroke noise) also fails closed instead of falling through to a
silent allow. Users who actually want to allow once still type one
of the documented values (`o`, `once`, `1`).

The match arms keep both the empty `""` and the catch-all `_` cases
explicit so a future contributor can't conflate them. Comment
documents the connection to the denial header so the invariant is
discoverable from the code.

files.patch:
@@ -812,8 +812,16 @@ fn select_allowlist_scope(timeout: Duration) -> Result<AllowlistScope, InputErro
                     Ok(AllowlistScope::Temporary(Duration::from_secs(24 * 3600)))
                 }
                 "p" | "permanent" | "perm" | "4" => Ok(AllowlistScope::Permanent),
-                "" => Ok(AllowlistScope::Once), // Default to once
-                _ => Ok(AllowlistScope::Once),  // Invalid input defaults to once (safest)
+                // Empty / unrecognized input must NOT silently allow.
+                // The denial UI tells the user `[Enter]` = "Keep blocked",
+                // and for `VerificationMethod::None` this prompt is the
+                // ONLY gate before allowing — defaulting to `Once` here
+                // turns Enter into a hands-free allow. Treat both cases
+                // as an explicit cancel so the caller (run_interactive_prompt)
+                // returns `InteractiveResult::Cancelled` and the command
+                // stays blocked.
+                "" => Err(InputError::Interrupted),
+                _ => Err(InputError::Interrupted),
             }
         }
         Err(err) => Err(err),
